resources.string: Meet Pausa.
Your timers, your way.
Eye Care 👀 : Every 20 minutes, pause and look 20 feet away for 20 seconds. Your eyes will thank you.
Focus 🧘 : Set it, forget it, get notified when it\'s done.
Quick ⏱️ : Need a 5-minute break? A 15-minute call? Just pick a time and go.
Designed to be fast. Launch timers from the notification itself — no app opening required.
Grant permission
Permission is granted
Finish
Next
Previous
I need to bug you.
(In the best way possible!)
Pausa sends you notifications when your timers finish or when it\'s time for an eye break. Without this permission, it\'s just a very quiet app sitting on your phone doing nothing.
Tap below to enable notifications. If you skip, you can always grant access later from Settings.
Punctuality is my thing.
(without it, I\'m basically useless!)
To ring exactly when scheduled, Pausa needs permission to set exact alarms. without it, Android may delay alarms, reminders, and timers when your device is idle
Tap below to enable alarms. If you skip, you can always grant access later from Settings.
Don\'t let timers sleep on the job.

Android\'s battery optimization can shut down background apps to save power. Since Pausa runs a foreground service 24/7 to keep your timers alive, it needs to be exempted from battery optimization. Otherwise, your timers might simply stop working.
This one\'s optional. The app works without it, just slightly less punctual.
